mechanism_narrative: A higher level of real GDP (GDPC1) relative to its long-term trend indicates above-potential output, which tightens resource utilization and elevates expectations for future short-term rates, thereby pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between GDPC1 level and 20-day forward yield change is negative (rank IC < 0) over any 3-year rolling window, or the average yield change following high-GDP readings is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals a sudden deterioration in labor demand, triggering a flight-to-safety into Treasuries and a downward repricing of expected policy rates, which pushes the 10-year yield lower over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points in a single month
failure_condition: In more than 40% of triggered months the 20-day forward yield rises, or the average 20-day yield change in triggered events is positive, over a rolling 2-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL above 2.5% year-over-year) and a tight labor market (UNRATE below 4.0%) produce overheating conditions that force the Federal Reserve to preemptively tighten policy, lifting real rate expectations and pushing the 10-year yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Fewer than 60% of months meeting the trigger are followed by a positive 20-day yield change, or the rank IC of the joint signal is negative over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is below 2%, any increase in the funds rate signals the start of a monetary normalization cycle. This raises expectations of higher short-term rates throughout the curve and increases term premiums, causing the 10-year yield to rise over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS below 2%, the correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and forward yield changes is negative (rank IC < 0) over any 2-year rolling window, or yield declines following increases more often than it rises.

mechanism_narrative: A large positive payroll surprise—monthly PAYEMS growth exceeding 200,000—signals accelerating labor demand and robust economic growth, which lifts near-term growth expectations and prompts a repricing of the short end of the yield curve, driving the 10-year yield higher over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of events where PAYEMS change exceeds 200k, the 5-day forward yield declines, or the average 5-day yield change is negative, over any 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) rises by more than 0.5% month-over-month while retail sales (RSAFS) are flat or declining, the economy is experiencing a supply-driven expansion without matching demand, which is disinflationary and reduces term premiums, pushing the 10-year yield lower over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.5% and RSAFS month-over-month growth ≤ 0%
failure_condition: Fewer than 60% of events satisfying the trigger are followed by a negative 20-day yield change, or the average yield change across such events is positive, over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic slack (industrial production INDPRO year-over-year growth negative), rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) does not signal overheating but instead reflects cost-push or transitory factors that are met with a dovish policy response, causing the 10-year yield to fall over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods of slack, the rank correlation between CPIAUCSL and forward yield change is positive (rank IC > 0) over any 2-year window, or yields rise following inflation increases more often than they fall.

mechanism_narrative: Higher levels of retail sales (RSAFS) reflect robust consumer spending, the main driver of aggregate demand. This signal raises expectations of sustained economic expansion and tighter future policy, exerting upward pressure on the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between RSAFS level and 20-day forward yield change is non-positive (rank IC ≤ 0) over any 3-year rolling window, or the average yield change in the highest RSAFS quintile is not significantly positive.
